Black Axe.description: The Black Axe is an international confraternity originating from Nigeria, founded in 1977 at the University of Benin. Originally a Pan-African student movement, it evolved into a criminal organization involved in cybercrime, human trafficking, drug trafficking, election fraud, and violence. The group has been described as a mafia-style gang and has been linked to the 1999 Obafemi Awolowo University massacre. Despite the Neo-Black Movement of Africa publicly distancing itself in 2024, the two are often considered synonymous.

Gambino crime family.description: The Gambino family is one of the Five Families of New York and one of the most powerful in the United States. Originating from the Black Hand gang led by Ignazio Lupo and Giuseppe Morello in East Harlem, it evolved under Salvatore D'Aquila and later Al Mineo. The family was formally established as one of the Five Families by Salvatore Maranzano after the Castellammarese War. It remains active in organized crime activities such as gambling and racketeering.

Omega.description: Omega is a Singaporean prison gang turned organized crime group, founded in 1989 in Chia Keng Prison by Yan Bai. It is a monoethnic Malay Muslim secret society formed to protect Malay inmates from discrimination by Chinese-dominated gangs. The group evolved into a criminal enterprise involved in drug trafficking and other illegal activities, with networks extending beyond Singapore.

Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubv.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organization and one of the emerging criminal gangs serving drug trafficking (Bacrim), part of the Colombian internal armed conflict. Founded in 2006 by Pedro Oliviero Guerrero Castillo alias 'Cuchillo' with 200 men, allied with drug trafficker Daniel Barrera alias 'El Loco Barrera'. After Cuchillo's death and Barrera's capture, 500 members surrendered in 2011, and the group split into two blocs. As of 2016, each bloc had 100 or fewer combatants.
